steps.title: Read only what the choices need
steps.content: The choices do not require the exact equation of the graphed line. They only ask for the signs of the slope and $y$-intercept of $f(x)$, because $c$ and $d$ are positive constants.

steps.title: Shift the graph down 19
steps.content: The graph shown is $y=f(x)+19$. To get the graph of $y=f(x)$, shift every point down $19$ units. The shown line has a positive $y$-intercept, but it is much less than $19$, so after shifting down $19$ units, the $y$-intercept of $f$ is negative.

steps.title: Keep the slope sign
steps.content: A vertical shift changes the intercept but does not change the slope. The shown line decreases from left to right, so $f$ also has a negative slope.

steps.title: Match the form
steps.content: With $c>0$ and $d>0$, the form $-d-cx$ has a negative $y$-intercept and a negative slope. That is exactly what the shifted graph of $f$ needs, so choice A is correct.
